releases.author: rixct
releases.description:
CodeRecall 1.0.1

Anki-style spaced repetition for code, inside Obsidian. Hide part of a code
block with a cloze, retype it from memory, and CodeRecall runs your code,
grades it against test cases, and schedules the next review with SM-2.

## Highlights
- Cloze code cards in ```coderecall blocks — header / code / tests split by
  `---`, so code is taken verbatim (indentation and tabs preserved).
- Runs and auto-grades your answer:
  - JavaScript — sandboxed Web Worker (offline)
  - Python — Pyodide / WASM (downloads once on first run)
  - C++ — local g++/clang++, full STL (falls back to a bundled JSCPP
    interpreter, no STL, when no compiler is found)
  - Java — local JDK 11+ (single-file mode)
- SM-2 scheduler with Again / Hard / Good / Easy; review state stored in data.json.
- Review modal: one input per cloze, Run & check, per-test pass/fail
  (expected vs got), and an auto verdict.
- In-note rendering: ```coderecall blocks show as compact cards with the code
  syntax-highlighted (toggle in settings).
- Settings: syntax highlighting, native execution (C++/Java), C++ compiler.

## Requirements
- Desktop only — C++/Java use local toolchains via Node.
- C++ needs g++ or clang++ on PATH; Java needs a JDK 11+. JavaScript and Python
  work out of the box.

## Install
- Manual: copy main.js, manifest.json and styles.css into
  <vault>/.obsidian/plugins/coderecall/ and enable the plugin.
- BRAT: add this repo, then enable CodeRecall.

See the README for the card format and a ready-made sample deck
(examples/sample-deck.md).
